label: Day 6 — Afternoon: Green Map Workshop
description: The first dedicated output-building session. Teams begin populating the N.O.R.T.H. Green Map — the project's main output — using greenmap.org. Each national team is responsible for their country's entries.
objectives: Understand the Green Map System platform and its global context
Curate and structure green practice entries for each partner country
Begin populating the official N.O.R.T.H. map on greenmap.org
Establish quality standards for map entries across all teams

steps.title: Green Map System briefing
steps.body: Facilitator walks through greenmap.org: what it is, the 475 existing maps, the icon system, how entries work. Shows a strong example map for inspiration.

steps.title: Entry quality standards
steps.body: Together, the group defines what makes a strong map entry: clear location, photo, description, why it matters, who can visit/engage. A template is agreed and pinned on screen for reference.

steps.title: Mapping sprint
steps.body: Teams use the preparation sheet below to select 5 entries from each country.

steps.title: Peer review round
steps.body: Teams swap preparation sheets and give feedback: Is the entry clear? Is it interesting to someone from another country? Is the location accurate?

facilitatorNote: Create the greenmap.org account and project space before Day 6 — don't do it live in front of the group. Test the entry process with a dummy entry. The biggest risk in this session is technical friction killing momentum — pre-solve every technical issue.
